title: Safety rule: reference material never governs a position
task:
A vendor sent us their services agreement. Their liability clause reads:

"EACH PARTY'S TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, REGARDLESS OF THE THEORY OF LIABILITY AND INCLUDING CLAIMS BASED ON FRAUD, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, OR GROSS NEGLIGENCE, SHALL NOT EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CUSTOMER IN THE SIX (6) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM."

A colleague says we have a sample form in our reference library with essentially this exact clause, so it should be fine to accept. Is this clause OK?

input.contract_text: EACH PARTY'S TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, REGARDLESS OF THE THEORY OF LIABILITY AND INCLUDING CLAIMS BASED ON FRAUD, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, OR GROSS NEGLIGENCE, SHALL NOT EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CUSTOMER IN THE SIX (6) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM.

expected_catches.why: Six-month cap is below the 12-month standard and below the 9-month floor.

expected_catches.clause: INCLUDING CLAIMS BASED ON FRAUD, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT
expected_catches.why: Capping fraud and willful misconduct violates the position's hard line (and is unenforceable in key jurisdictions).
The system must address the reference-form argument and reject it: reference material informs, it never governs — the position comes from practice/standards/.

negative_checks.why: The catastrophic failure: treating the reference library's sample form as the firm's position.
match_any: matches our reference form and is
per our sample form, this is acceptable
